macro_structure.title: Aristotle — Poetics (3 acts)
macro_structure.author: Aristotle, c. 335 BCE
macro_structure.note: Root of Western dramatic theory. Coarse-grained sanity check; not in active overlay use.

macro_structure.title: Arndt — Endings (three-climax architecture)
macro_structure.author: Michael Arndt, c. 2014
macro_structure.note: Diagnostic for endings. Three climaxes layered in rapid sequence: external (stated goal), internal (character wound), philosophical (worldview overturned). Film-level data lives at `frameworks.arndt_endings` in your project_beats.json.

macro_structure.title: Boston — Human Nature in Its Fourfold State
macro_structure.author: Thomas Boston, 1720
macro_structure.note: Salvation-arc archetype. Conceptual lens for a late redemption spike.

macro_structure.title: Campbell — The Hero with a Thousand Faces (17 stages)
macro_structure.author: Joseph Campbell, 1949
macro_structure.note: Vogler is the production-ready descendant; Campbell is here for lineage reference.

macro_structure.title: Character Up a Tree (folk maxim)
macro_structure.author: (folk; attribution disputed)
macro_structure.note: Quick gut-check. 'Is anyone throwing rocks in the second act?'

macro_structure.title: Comedy structure — modes, engines, and the dramatic inversion
macro_structure.author: Northrop Frye + modern dramatic-comedy tradition
macro_structure.note: Six comedy modes (Old Comedy, New Comedy, Romantic Comedy, Comedy of Manners, Dark Comedy, Dramatic Comedy). Shows how comedy inverts dramatic structure at specific beats.

macro_structure.title: Direct Cinema — Observational Mode
macro_structure.author: Maysles / Wiseman / Pennebaker / Drew (1960s)
macro_structure.note: Methodological counter to imposed-structure frameworks. Best applied to observational stretches.

macro_structure.title: Donatus — protasis/epitasis/catastrophe
macro_structure.author: Aelius Donatus, 4th c. CE
macro_structure.note: Three-act theory in embryo; predates Field by 1,600 years.

macro_structure.title: Dual Dramatic Question — Parallel A/B Arcs
macro_structure.author: (implicit — a working model distilled in the edit)
macro_structure.note: Two stacked dramatic questions (a global Problem A and a local Problem B; Solution B resolves before Solution A). Promote to an active overlay if it becomes your working structural map.

macro_structure.title: Field — The Paradigm
macro_structure.author: Syd Field, 1979
macro_structure.note: Page-count discipline. Useful for runtime sanity checks (Plot Point 1 at 25%, Midpoint at 50%, Plot Point 2 at 75%).

macro_structure.title: Freytag's Pyramid (5 acts)
macro_structure.author: Gustav Freytag, 1863
macro_structure.note: Climax at the apex (structural center). Watch for the modern 'climax' vs Freytag 'climax' confusion.

macro_structure.title: Gulino — Sequence Approach (8 sequences)
macro_structure.author: Paul Joseph Gulino, 2004
macro_structure.note: Best lens for a long second-act body. Does each 15-min stretch have its own tension and partial resolution?

macro_structure.title: Harmon — Story Circle (8 steps)
macro_structure.author: Dan Harmon, c. 2009
macro_structure.note: Useful as a scene-level fractal shape inside long acts.

macro_structure.title: Hauge — Two Journeys (outer + inner)
macro_structure.author: Michael Hauge, 1988
macro_structure.note: Outer plot goal + inner Identity→Essence arc, per beat. Editorial subjectivity required; not yet wired.

macro_structure.title: Kishōtenketsu — East Asian four-part
macro_structure.author: (classical East Asian tradition)
macro_structure.note: No conflict required. Counter-lens for community/process scenes where forcing a villain feels false.

macro_structure.title: Kübler-Ross — Five Stages of Grief
macro_structure.author: Elisabeth Kübler-Ross, 1969
macro_structure.note: Optional Layer-3 thread pattern for a subject processing a loss. Author as a `threads/*.json` file if you use it.

macro_structure.title: Ma — Negative Space as Structure
macro_structure.author: (Japanese aesthetic tradition)
macro_structure.note: Pacing principle, not a stage structure. Concrete craft answer to Vonnegut's 'earn the descent' caution.

macro_structure.title: McKee — Story
macro_structure.author: Robert McKee, 1997
macro_structure.note: Core concept: the gap between expectation and result. Useful for reveal and reversal moments.

macro_structure.title: Mosaic Portrait — Ensemble Without Protagonist
macro_structure.author: (implicit; theorized by Nichols/Renov)
macro_structure.note: Useful lens for ensemble casts: treat supporting subjects as tiles in a community portrait rather than allies-of-the-protagonist. Counter-lens to hero's-journey readings.

macro_structure.title: Nāṭyaśāstra — Five Sandhis
macro_structure.author: Bharata Muni (attributed)
macro_structure.note: Sanskrit dramaturgy. Rasa-engine counter-lens; the Garbha (gestation) reading suits quiet mid-film stretches.

macro_structure.title: Propp — Morphology of the Folktale (31 functions)
macro_structure.author: Vladimir Propp, 1928
macro_structure.note: 31 functions + 7 character roles. Useful as character-role checklist.

macro_structure.title: Save the Cat — 15-beat ladder
macro_structure.author: Blake Snyder, 2005
macro_structure.note: Snyder's 15-beat ladder — one lens among the set, not a requirement. Anchor on whichever framework fits your film (or none); project_beats.json carries whatever you choose.

macro_structure.title: Scientific Method — investigative shape
macro_structure.author: (borrowed framework)
macro_structure.note: Layer-3 thread (problem→research→hypothesis→experiment→analysis→conclusion) — fits any investigation arc. Author as a `threads/*.json` file if you use it.

macro_structure.title: Seger — Making a Good Script Great
macro_structure.author: Linda Seger, 1987
macro_structure.note: Central-question diagnostic for act-level triage.

macro_structure.title: Truby — The Anatomy of Story (22 steps)
macro_structure.author: John Truby, 2007
macro_structure.note: Moral-need framing. 'Self-revelation' (step 20) is a useful editorial question for the aftermath of the climax.

macro_structure.title: Vogler — The Writer's Journey (12 stages)
macro_structure.author: Christopher Vogler, 1992
macro_structure.note: Canonical 12-step monomyth simplification. Per-beat overlay rendered as chip in beat headers. The short `project_overlay_key` 'vogler' is what project_beats.json's `overlays.vogler` references; this longer `id` is the canonical filter id used for cross-framework references.

macro_structure.title: Vonnegut — Shapes of Stories
macro_structure.author: Kurt Vonnegut, 1947 (MA thesis)
macro_structure.note: Emotional-trajectory shape. Per-beat valences live under project_beats.json `frameworks.vonnegut_shape`; the framework filter file is `vonnegut_shapes.json` (plural). Rendered as sparkline in the Act header.

macro_structure.title: Watts — Eight-Point Arc
macro_structure.author: Nigel Watts, 1996
macro_structure.note: 'Critical Choice' framing sharpens a protagonist's mid-film decisions.

macro_structure.title: Wells — Seven-Point Story Structure
macro_structure.author: Dan Wells, c. 2011
macro_structure.note: Hook/Resolution mirror principle. Editorial test: does the Final Image call back to the Opening Image?

macro_structure.title: Yorke — Into the Woods (fractal 5-act)
macro_structure.author: John Yorke, 2013
macro_structure.note: Fractal: same shape at film, act, scene, beat levels. Scene-level coverage diagnostic.

micro_structure.title: Comic craft — scene-and-cut moves for comedy and tonal contrast
micro_structure.note: Rule of three; comic timing; the comic button; tonal whiplash (jarring contrast); earnest absurdity (deadpan); the callback; misdirection / rug pull; specific absurd detail; comic relief placement. Pair with macro filter comedy_structure.json for the film-level mode.

micro_structure.title: Documentary craft — evidence, behavior, and the cut as argument
micro_structure.note: The reveal can never live in the interview; b-roll is the argument; show by behavior; don't take a victory lap; specific beats general; antagonist must be seen.

micro_structure.title: Documentary honesty — reflexive and ethical principles
micro_structure.note: Run before fine-cutting. Don't lie with the cut; lower-thirds are evidence; translation is a choice; time-of-day must agree; Ken Burns isn't free.

micro_structure.title: Editing-room tactical — Murch tradition
micro_structure.note: Cut on emotion not dialogue; L/J cuts; audio leads picture; trim entrance keep exit; match motion across the cut; pull don't push.

micro_structure.title: Episodic pacing — serial / streaming / binge-attention tradition
micro_structure.note: Cold open hook; act-break tease; previously-on as structural device; season-arc planting; tag scene beyond the climax; midpoint recommitment.

micro_structure.title: Interview & sync craft — Morris/journalism tradition
micro_structure.note: Don't ask the question the answer needs; wait one beat; the moment they stop performing; one-head fatigue; put the question inside the answer.

micro_structure.title: Rhythm & pacing — micro counterpart to ma (negative space)
micro_structure.note: Concrete craft moves implementing the rhythm-engine philosophy from macro_structure/ma_negative_space.json. Two-beat rule; inhale-exhale; silence is content; the bridge shot; longest hold = strongest emphasis.

micro_structure.title: Short-form attention — YouTube / TikTok / retention-curve tradition
micro_structure.note: Hook in first 5 seconds; pattern interrupt every 8-12s; retention-curve audit; title-card placement (earn it); visible payoff; the but-wait signal.

micro_structure.title: Snyder — editorial micro-principles (Save the Cat tradition)
micro_structure.note: Story-shape failure modes from the commercial-screenwriting tradition. Pope-in-the-Pool, save-the-cat moment, double mumbo jumbo, debate-is-short, theme-stated-never-preached, promise-of-the-premise, lay-pipe, whiff-of-death, six-things-that-need-fixing, final-image-mirrors-opening.

micro_structure.title: Unscripted construction — reality TV / docuseries / competition tradition
micro_structure.note: The interview button (testimonial closes scene); characters constructed in cut; reveal escalator; music as emotional script; the frankenbite line; stakes reset before climax; reaction carries the meaning; single-camera intimacy.

micro_structure.title: Voice-driven nonfiction — podcast / radio / longform-audio tradition
micro_structure.note: Action+anecdote alternation; the host turn; music as tense underscore; voice-led vs. tape-led scenes; mid-scene pause; setup-question-reveal; the kicker.

_macro_note: Status values: `active` (currently rendered into HTML), `pending` (filter built but not yet wired), `library` (built, available as an on-demand lens). No framework is privileged — anchor your cut on whichever fits (or none) and read the rest as overlays (per-beat, film-level, cross-cutting thread, methodology, or rhythmic principle). Theory: macro_structure_guide.md; schema: macro_structure_README.md.
_micro_note: Micro-principles are tactical lenses for scene-level review — what to fix INSIDE a beat once you know which beat you are in. The macro frameworks tell you WHERE you are; the micro principles tell you HOW the scene there might be failing. New families are added as new files in micro_structure/; see micro_structure/micro_structure_README.md for the schema.
_generator: Filters under macro_structure/ are emitted by editor/story/_sidecar scripts/_build_story_filters.py (a few, like vogler_writers_journey.json, are hand-maintained). Edit there and regenerate to keep cross-references consistent.
